tasks.description: Playbook mode — distraction-free runbook execution view with step-by-step navigation, progress indicator, and mobile-responsive layout
acceptance_criteria: New route at /w/[workspaceId]/playbook?runbookId=xxx — distraction-free view (no sidebar, no header, no tab bar)
Shows current step large/centered with step name, description (from original step), assigned person, tools, tasks
Mark Complete & Next button advances to next pending/in_progress step
Progress indicator shows step N of M
Prev/Next navigation arrows to move between steps
Completed steps show green checkmark + completion timestamp
URL is shareable (runbookId in query param or path)
Back link to return to full runbook view
Sidebar nav item added for Playbook (or link from runbook view)

patterns_to_follow: RunbookView component pattern — server page.tsx fetches data, client view component renders
RunbookStepEnriched type with joined step data
STEP_STATUS_CONFIG and STEP_STATUS_ORDER patterns from runbook-view.tsx
workspace-shell.tsx line 47: reserved paths array — add 'playbook'
sidebar.tsx NAV_ITEMS pattern — add Playbook entry with Play/BookOpen icon
sidebar.tsx Workflows active function — exclude /playbook path
learnings_relevant: React 19 useRef requires initial value: useRef(null)
apiFetch unwraps envelope — returns T directly
Lucide-react has no title prop — use aria-label

tasks.special_instructions: This is attempt 2 — attempt 1 code was lost in pipeline merge failure (iter 84). The view should be DISTRACTION-FREE: no sidebar, no header, no tab bar — just the playbook content with a back button. Use the existing runbook data model (runbooks + runbook_steps tables). Server page.tsx fetches runbook + steps by runbookId query param, renders PlaybookView client component. The PlaybookView shows one step at a time, large and centered, with Mark Complete & Next as the primary action. Do NOT add a sidebar nav item for Playbook — it's accessed via a link from the runbook view page, not from the sidebar. Update workspace-shell.tsx reserved paths and sidebar Workflows exclusion only.

tasks.description: Replace window.confirm() with styled Radix Dialog in runbook Complete/Cancel buttons
acceptance_criteria: handleCompleteRunbook uses styled Radix Dialog instead of window.confirm()
handleCancelRunbook uses styled Radix Dialog instead of window.confirm()
Complete dialog explains consequence: completing will lock all steps and mark runbook as done
Cancel dialog explains consequence: cancelling will stop the runbook
Dialogs match dark theme design system (dark background, proper text hierarchy, brand/destructive button colors)
Dialog has Cancel and Confirm buttons with appropriate variants
Zero window.confirm() calls remain in runbook-view.tsx

patterns_to_follow: Existing Radix Dialog usage in the codebase (dialog.tsx is already a wrapped Radix primitive)
Button component variants: default (blue accent), destructive, ghost
learnings_relevant: Radix UI primitives are already in use (dialog, dropdown-menu, etc.)

tasks.special_instructions: This is attempt 2 — attempt 1 code was lost in pipeline merge failure (iter 84). Import Dialog, DialogContent, DialogHeader, DialogTitle, DialogDescription, DialogFooter from @/components/ui/dialog. Use controlled open state (useState) to manage dialog visibility. Two separate dialogs: one for Complete (primary action, brand color), one for Cancel (destructive action, red). Each dialog includes a consequence sentence explaining what will happen.
